## 1. Executive Summary

### What is Soul Yatri?
Soul Yatri is an ambitious mental wellness platform aiming to connect users with therapists, AI-powered wellness tools, courses, community support, and holistic health tracking. Built with React 19 + Express + PostgreSQL (Prisma), it targets the Indian mental health market.

### Current State: Score 31/100

| Dimension | Score | Status |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| Frontend UI | 77/100 | Good visual quality, 4 pages lack dark mode |
| Frontend Functionality | 42/100 | 49% of pages API-connected; 30% mock data; 21% static |
| Backend API | 28/100 | 35% real endpoints; 65% return 501 |
| Database | 25/100 | 20 models defined; only 6 actively used |
| Security | 12/100 | CRITICAL: Auth bypass enabled in production |
| Testing | 8/100 | 1 spec file with 2 smoke tests; zero unit tests |
| Documentation | 35/100 | Extensive but 40-60% inaccurate (drift) |
| DevOps | 55/100 | Docker + CI/CD configured; missing test execution |
| SEO | 5/100 | No meta tags, no sitemap, no schema.org |
| Payments | 0/100 | All 27 endpoints return 501 |
| AI Features | 0/100 | All 11 endpoints return 501 |
| Video/Telehealth | 0/100 | No video SDK integrated |
| **OVERALL** | **31/100** | **Early prototype with excellent UI shell** |

### The One-Sentence Verdict
> Soul Yatri has a **beautiful frontend shell** with professional design system (shadcn/ui + Tailwind + Framer Motion), a **well-structured backend skeleton**, but **critical security vulnerabilities** and **65% of the backend is stub code** — it's an impressive demo that needs 8-12 weeks of focused development to become a launchable product.

### Top 5 Immediate Actions
1. **FIX AUTH BYPASS** — Set `authBypassEnabled` default to `false` (10 minutes)
2. **FIX DEV LOGIN** — Guard with `NODE_ENV !== 'production'` (5 minutes)
3. **WIRE CONTACT FORM** — Primary lead capture is completely broken (2 hours)
4. **INTEGRATE RAZORPAY** — Zero revenue possible without payments (3-5 days)
5. **INSTALL DEPENDENCIES + BUILD** — Verify the app actually compiles (1-2 hours)

---

## 2. Audit Methodology

### Scope
- **4,684 total files** inventoried
- **416 files** semantically reviewed (deep code read)
- **All** source code files (TypeScript, TSX, JavaScript, CSS, JSON, YAML)
- **All** documentation files (Markdown)
- **All** configuration files

### Process
| Batch | Area | Files Read | Method |
|-------|------|-----------|--------|
| 0 | Bootstrap & Inventory | All | File listing + extension analysis |
| 1-2 | Frontend Architecture | 30+ | Deep read of routes, auth, state, layouts |
| 3 | Frontend Pages | 35+ | Every page file read and classified |
| 4-5 | Backend | 50+ | Every route, controller, service, middleware |
| 6 | Database | 15+ | Schema, migrations, seeds, models |
| 7 | Tests & Scripts | 10+ | Test files, CI/CD, Docker |
| 8 | Documentation | 20+ | Every doc file compared to code reality |
| 10 | Assets & Config | 15+ | Images, Tailwind, Vite, TypeScript configs |

### Limitations
- **Build not verified**: `node_modules` not installed; `npm run build` could not be executed
- **No runtime testing**: App could not be started to verify UI visually
- **No screenshot audit**: Pages assessed from code analysis only
- **Integration pricing**: Based on published pricing pages (may change)

### Source Code Lines (estimated)
| Area | Files | Est. Lines |
|------|-------|-----------|
| Frontend (src/) | ~180 | ~25,000 |
| Backend (server/) | ~70 | ~8,000 |
| Config files | ~20 | ~1,000 |
| Documentation | ~30 | ~15,000 |
| **Total Code** | ~270 | **~34,000** |
| **Total Docs** | ~30 | **~15,000** |

### Monorepo Structure
```
soul-yatri-website/
├── src/                    # React frontend (Vite)
│   ├── components/         # Shared UI components (shadcn/ui)
│   ├── config/             # Runtime config, permissions, routes
│   ├── constants/          # App constants
│   ├── context/            # React Context (Auth, Theme)
│   ├── features/           # Feature modules (constellation, onboarding, etc.)
│   ├── hooks/              # Custom React hooks
│   ├── layouts/            # Layout components (Main, Dashboard, Auth)
│   ├── lib/                # Utility libraries
│   ├── middleware/          # Route guards (ProtectedRoute)
│   ├── pages/              # Page components (~35 pages)
│   ├── router/             # React Router configuration
│   ├── services/           # API client services
│   ├── types/              # TypeScript type definitions
│   └── utils/              # Utility functions
├── server/                 # Express backend
│   ├── prisma/             # Database schema + migrations
│   └── src/
│       ├── config/         # Server configuration
│       ├── controllers/    # Route handlers (ACTIVE)
│       ├── lib/            # Shared libraries
│       ├── middleware/      # Express middleware
│       ├── modules/        # ABANDONED module stubs
│       ├── routes/         # Route definitions
│       ├── services/       # Business logic services
│       ├── shared/         # Shared types/utilities
│       └── validators/     # Zod validation schemas
├── public/                 # Static assets (images)
├── tests/                  # Playwright E2E tests
├── docs/                   # Documentation
└── scripts/                # Build/audit scripts
```

**Detailed inventory**: See [docs/audit/00_repo_inventory.md](audit/00_repo_inventory.md)

| Category | Count | Examples |

| Protected Routes | 15 | Dashboard, Mood, Journal, Meditation, Profile, Settings, Sessions |

| Practitioner Routes | 5 | Practitioner Dashboard, Today's Sessions, My Clients, Availability |
| **Total** | **35** | |

### Page Implementation Status
| Status | Count | % | Pages |
|--------|-------|---|-------|
| LIVE (API-connected) | 12 | 34% | Dashboard, Mood, Journal, Meditation, Confessional, Notifications, Profile, Settings, Login, Signup, Logout, Constellation |
| MOCK (hardcoded data) | 14 | 40% | Blogs, BlogPost, Courses, CourseDetail, Career, Admin, Practitioner, Sessions, Connections, etc. |
| STATIC (no data needed) | 9 | 26% | Landing, About, Business, Contact, StudentCounselling, Workshop, Splash, 404, Onboarding |
| **Total** | **35** | **100%** | |

### State Management
| Approach | Used | Scope |
|----------|------|-------|
| React Context | ✅ AuthContext, ThemeContext | Global |
| Local State (useState) | ✅ Everywhere | Per-component |
| URL State (searchParams) | ✅ Blog filters, course filters | Per-page |
| React Query | ❌ Not installed | — |
| Zustand | ❌ Not installed | — |
| Redux | ❌ Not installed | — |

**Verdict**: State management is minimal but functional. React Query would be the single highest-impact addition for data fetching.

### Service Layer Reality

| Service | Status | Implementation |
|---------|--------|---------------|
| Token Service | ✅ REAL | JWT sign/verify/refresh |
| Auth Controller | ✅ REAL | Login, signup, refresh, logout, password reset |
| User Controller | ✅ REAL | CRUD, profile, avatar, role management |
| Health Tools Controller | ✅ REAL | Mood, journal, meditation, confessional CRUD |
| Notification Controller | ✅ REAL | WebSocket + DB notifications |
| Cache Service | ⚠️ STUB | In-memory Map (no Redis) |
| Email Service | ⚠️ STUB | console.log only |
| Queue Service | ⚠️ STUB | Synchronous execution |
| Storage Service | ⚠️ STUB | In-memory (files lost on restart) |
| AI Event Logger | ✅ REAL | Winston-based event logging |

### Duplicate Architecture Problem
The codebase has TWO controller patterns:
1. **`server/src/controllers/*.ts`** (12 files) — ACTIVE, used by all route files
2. **`server/src/modules/*/controller.ts`** (18 dirs) — ABANDONED, all empty/stub

**Recommendation**: Delete the entire `server/src/modules/` directory.

### Full endpoint matrix: See [docs/audit/03_backend_route_matrix.csv](audit/03_backend_route_matrix.csv)

| Achievement | ⚠️ ORPHAN | Defined but unused | name, description, icon, criteria | — |
| UserAchievement | ⚠️ ORPHAN | Defined but unused | userId, achievementId, unlockedAt | User, Achievement |
| CommunityPost | ⚠️ PARTIAL | Schema exists, stubs only | title, content, authorId, likes | User |
| CommunityComment | ⚠️ PARTIAL | Schema exists, stubs only | content, postId, authorId | CommunityPost, User |
| Event | ⚠️ ORPHAN | Schema exists, stubs only | title, description, date, capacity | User |
| Membership | ⚠️ ORPHAN | Schema exists, stubs only | tier, userId, startDate, endDate, amount | User |

## 8. Authentication & Security

### CRITICAL VULNERABILITIES

| ID | Vulnerability | Severity | CVSS Est. | Status |
|----|--------------|----------|-----------|--------|
| SEC-01 | Auth bypass enabled by default | CRITICAL | 9.8 | OPEN |
| SEC-02 | Mock auth with hardcoded credentials in prod | CRITICAL | 9.1 | OPEN |
| SEC-03 | Dev login routes enabled in production | CRITICAL | 8.8 | OPEN |
| SEC-04 | Client-side btoa() labeled as password hashing | HIGH | 7.5 | OPEN |
| SEC-05 | Docker-compose hardcoded JWT secret | HIGH | 7.2 | OPEN |
| SEC-06 | Docker-compose hardcoded DB password | HIGH | 7.2 | OPEN |
| SEC-07 | CSRF token generated but never validated | MEDIUM | 5.3 | OPEN |
| SEC-08 | No AES-256 encryption despite claims | MEDIUM | 5.0 | OPEN (no PHI stored yet) |
| SEC-09 | No audit logging despite model existing | LOW | 3.5 | OPEN |

### SEC-01 Detail: Auth Bypass
```
Location: src/config/runtime.flags.ts
Line: authBypassEnabled defaults to true
Effect: ProtectedRoute.tsx returns <Outlet /> without auth check
Impact: ANY page accessible without login, including admin
Fix: Change default to false
Time to fix: 10 minutes
```

### SEC-03 Detail: Dev Login
```
Location: server/src/routes/dev-login.ts
Endpoints:
  GET /api/dev-login/admin@test.com → Returns admin JWT
  GET /api/dev-login/therapist@test.com → Returns therapist JWT
  GET /api/dev-login/user@test.com → Returns user JWT
Effect: Anyone can GET a URL and receive a valid JWT token
Impact: Full admin access without credentials
Fix: Guard with NODE_ENV check
Time to fix: 5 minutes
```

### Auth Flow (when working correctly)
1. User submits email + password to POST `/api/auth/login`
2. Server validates with bcrypt, issues JWT (access + refresh tokens)
3. Access token stored in memory; refresh token in httpOnly cookie
4. Frontend sends access token in Authorization header
5. Server middleware validates JWT on protected routes
6. Token rotation on refresh

### Security Score Breakdown
| Area | Score |
|------|-------|
| Authentication mechanism | 70/100 (JWT + bcrypt + refresh rotation — solid when bypass disabled) |
| Authorization | 20/100 (role-based defined but bypass makes it meaningless) |
| Input validation | 60/100 (Zod on some endpoints; missing on many) |
| CSRF protection | 15/100 (token generated, never validated) |
| Rate limiting | 50/100 (global limit exists; not per-role/endpoint) |
| Secrets management | 20/100 (hardcoded in docker-compose) |
| Encryption | 5/100 (no field-level encryption) |
| Audit logging | 5/100 (model exists, nothing writes to it) |
| **OVERALL SECURITY** | **12/100** |

## 9. UI/UX & Design System

### Design System Score: 77/100

| Dimension | Score | Notes |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| Color Tokens | 80 | Good CSS variable system; 4 pages bypass with hardcoded hex |
| Typography | 65 | Consistent Tailwind classes; no custom brand font |
| Spacing | 90 | Excellent Tailwind scale usage |
| Component Library | 85 | 22 shadcn/ui components; CVA variants; well-structured |
| Layout System | 85 | 3 layout types (Main, Dashboard, Auth); responsive sidebar |
| Responsive Design | 82 | Mobile-first; all 4 breakpoints used; touch-friendly |
| Animation System | 78 | 6 animation libraries (GSAP, Framer, Lottie, AOS, anime.js, CSS) |
| Accessibility | 55 | Basic semantic HTML + ARIA; missing skip-to-content, prefers-reduced-motion |

### Visual Highlights
- Professional landing page with GSAP ScrollTrigger parallax
- Consistent card-based design language
- Smooth page transitions via Framer Motion
- Dark mode support (except 4 pages)
- Emoji-based mood picker (delightful UX)
- Time-based dashboard greeting (Good Morning/Afternoon/Evening)

### Problem Areas
- 4 pages hardcode light-mode colors
- Contact form is the primary lead-capture page but has non-functional buttons
- 6+ animation libraries contribute to bundle size
- No custom brand font (using system fonts)
- No skip-to-content link for accessibility
- No prefers-reduced-motion support

### Full visual matrix: See [docs/audit/08_ui_ux_visual_matrix.csv](audit/08_ui_ux_visual_matrix.csv)

## 10. CTA & Conversion Analysis

### CTA Inventory: 50+ Buttons Audited

| Status | Count | % |
|--------|-------|---|
| Functional | 28 | 56% |
| Partially Functional | 8 | 16% |
| Non-Functional | 14 | 28% |

### Critical Broken CTAs (Direct Revenue/Lead Impact)

| Page | CTA | Impact | Fix Effort |
|------|-----|--------|-----------|
| Contact | "Send Request" | Lead capture = ZERO | 2 hours |
| Career (x11) | "Apply" | Hiring = ZERO | 2 hours |
| Sessions | "Book Session" | Therapy revenue = ZERO | 2-3 weeks |
| Connections | "Connect" | Matching = ZERO | 1-2 weeks |
| Astrology | "Generate Chart" | Feature demo = broken | 1-2 weeks |
| Workshop | "Register Interest" | Event signup = ZERO | 2 hours |
| Business (x2) | "Contact Sales" / "Schedule Demo" | Corporate leads → broken contact page | 2 hours |

### Conversion Funnel Gaps
```
Landing Page → "Start Your Journey" → Contact Page → "Send Request" → DEAD END
                                                      ↑ BROKEN: No handler

Landing Page → "Explore" → Contact Page → DEAD END

Career Page → "Apply" → DEAD END (no handler)

Course Page → "Enroll" → Login → Dashboard → "Book Session" → DEAD END (mock data)
```

### Full CTA audit: See [docs/audit/09_cta_button_audit.csv](audit/09_cta_button_audit.csv)

## 11. Feature Completeness Matrix

### Scoring Methodology
Each feature scored 0-100 across 21 dimensions:
- Frontend (UI exists), Backend (API works), Database (models/data), Integration (3rd party)
- Testing (coverage), Docs (accuracy), DevOps (deployable), Security (auth/encryption)
- SEO, UI quality, UX quality, Design system compliance, CTA effectiveness
- Content quality, Accessibility, Performance, Conversion effectiveness
- Functionality (end-to-end works), Overall weighted score

### Top 10 Features by Score

| Rank | Feature | Score | Status |
|------|---------|-------|--------|
| 1 | User Authentication | 72 | Strongest feature (auth bypass aside) |
| 2 | Mood Tracking | 68 | Real CRUD, emoji picker, API-connected |
| 3 | Journal System | 65 | Real CRUD, rich entries, API-connected |
| 4 | Meditation Logging | 62 | Timer, ambient sounds, session logging |
| 5 | Notifications | 58 | WebSocket real-time, mark read, clear all |
| 6 | User Profile | 55 | Basic CRUD, avatar, settings |
| 7 | Landing Page | 52 | Beautiful UI but CTA links to broken contact |
| 8 | Confessional | 50 | Anonymous confessions, reactions |
| 9 | Dark Mode | 48 | Works on most pages; 4 pages missing |
| 10 | Error Boundary | 45 | Custom 404; global error boundary exists |

### Bottom 10 Features by Score

| Rank | Feature | Score | Status |
|------|---------|-------|--------|
| 33 | Astrology | 5 | Beautiful dashboard with zero backend |
| 34 | Events | 5 | Model exists; everything else stub |
| 35 | NGO Partnership | 3 | Routes only; no UI, no data |
| 36 | Corporate Wellness | 3 | Static page; no backend |
| 37 | E-Commerce/Shop | 2 | 15 endpoints all 501; no models |
| 38 | Video/Telehealth | 0 | No SDK, no integration |
| 39 | Push Notifications | 0 | No FCM, no service worker |

| 41 | Payments | 0 | 27 endpoints all 501; no Razorpay |

## 12. Gap Analysis

### Gap Severity Distribution

| Severity | Count | Key Gaps |
|----------|-------|----------|
| CRITICAL | 5 | Payment system, therapy booking, auth bypass, mock auth, dev login |
| HIGH | 9 | Admin dashboard, AI, blog schema, course schema, video, email, contact form, cache, storage |
| MEDIUM | 7 | SEO, OAuth, practitioner onboarding, constellation, connections, Docker secrets, btoa() |

### Dead Code & Duplication

| ID | Type | Files | Impact |
|----|------|-------|--------|
| DUP-001 | Duplicate controller architecture | 18 dirs | Confusion; maintenance burden |
| DUP-002 | Duplicate practitioner dashboard | 2 files | Route confusion |
| DUP-003 | 4 redundant seed scripts | 4 files | One is broken; passwords inconsistent |
| DUP-004 | Duplicate setup scripts | 2 files | Same logic in CJS + MJS |
| DUP-005 | Duplicate test routes | 2 files | Unnecessary |

### Full gap register: See [docs/audit/06_gap_register.md](audit/06_gap_register.md)

---

## 13. Documentation Drift Register

### Summary: 21 Drift Items Found

| Severity | Count | Examples |
|----------|-------|---------|
| CRITICAL | 8 | React Query, Zustand, AES-256, HIPAA logging, Socket.IO, Sentry, PostHog, multi-currency |
| MAJOR | 8 | Phase overclaims, Redis, video, AI, test suite, notifications, community, shop |
| MINOR | 5 | "Enterprise-grade" claims, rate limiting scope, avatar upload, i18n, PWA |

### Root Cause
BUILD_PLAN.md is a **3,785-line aspirational document** that doesn't distinguish between "implemented" and "planned." Every AI agent or developer reading it will assume capabilities exist that don't.

### Fix
Split into `CURRENT_STATE.md` (what works) + `ROADMAP.md` (what's planned). Add status badges: ✅ LIVE | 🚧 WIP | 📋 PLANNED | 💡 VISION

### Full drift register: See [docs/audit/07_documentation_drift.md](audit/07_documentation_drift.md)

---

## 16. Performance & Bundle Analysis

### Build Configuration (Vite)
- ✅ Code splitting via React.lazy
- ✅ Manual chunks defined in vite.config.ts (vendor, ui, animation, charts)
- ✅ Bundle budget script exists (`scripts/check-bundle-budgets.js`)
- ✅ Compression configured (gzip)
- ⚠️ Build not verified (node_modules missing)

### Potential Bundle Concerns
| Library | Est. Size (gzipped) | Justification |
|---------|---------------------|---------------|
| Three.js | ~150KB | Used only in constellation feature |
| GSAP | ~30KB | Landing page animations only |
| Framer Motion | ~35KB | Used widely — justified |
| Recharts | ~45KB | Dashboard charts — justified |
| anime.js | ~15KB | Could remove (Framer Motion covers same) |
| AOS | ~5KB | Could remove (Framer Motion covers same) |
| lenis | ~10KB | Smooth scroll — nice but optional |
| zxcvbn | ~400KB | Password strength — TREE SHAKE or lazy load |

### Performance Recommendations
1. **Lazy-load Three.js** — only used in /constellation
2. **Lazy-load zxcvbn** — only needed on signup page (400KB savings)
3. **Remove anime.js and AOS** — Framer Motion handles both use cases
4. **Add Lighthouse CI** to GitHub Actions
5. **Add Core Web Vitals monitoring** via PostHog

### Performance Score: 60/100 (estimated, build not verified)

| Element | Status | Notes |

| Canonical URLs | ❌ Missing | No `<link rel="canonical">` |
| Sitemap.xml | ❌ Missing | No sitemap generation |
| robots.txt | ❌ Missing | No robots.txt file |
| Schema.org | ❌ Missing | No structured data |
| Alt Tags | ⚠️ Partial | Some images have alt; many don't |
| Heading Hierarchy | ✅ Good | Proper H1-H6 usage |
| URL Structure | ✅ Good | Clean, descriptive URLs |
| SSR/SSG | ❌ SPA Only | Client-side rendering = poor initial SEO |
| react-helmet-async | ❌ Not installed | No per-page meta tags |

### SEO Score: 5/100

### Quick Fix: Install react-helmet-async and add meta tags to each page.

## 19. Integration & Platform Options

### Recommended Stack for Production

| Category | Recommended | Monthly Cost | Why |
|----------|-------------|-------------|-----|
| Payments | Razorpay | 2% per txn | INR native; UPI first-class |
| Video | Daily.co | Free-$12/mo | HIPAA BAA; easy embed |
| AI | OpenAI GPT-4o-mini | $3-15/mo | Best price/quality |
| Email | Resend | Free-$20/mo | Simplest DX; React Email |
| Storage | Cloudflare R2 | Free-$5/mo | Zero egress; S3-compatible |
| Cache | Upstash Redis | Free-$10/mo | Serverless; REST API |
| DB | Neon Postgres | Free-$69/mo | Serverless; scale-to-zero |
| Errors | Sentry | Free-$26/mo | Industry standard |
| Analytics | PostHog | Free | 1M events/mo; self-hostable |
| Frontend | Vercel | Free-$20/mo | Already configured |
| Backend | Railway | $5-$20/mo | Docker-ready |
| Search | Meilisearch | Free-$30/mo | Typo-tolerant; faceted |
| Monitoring | BetterUptime | Free-$20/mo | Status page included |

### Full comparison: See [docs/audit/10_integration_options_matrix.csv](audit/10_integration_options_matrix.csv)

| Production Scale | 5,000-10,000 | ₹49,790 | ₹6.6L/mo (92.5% margin) |

### Unit Economics at Scale

- Infrastructure cost per paying user: ₹34
- Break-even: 100 Basic members (₹499/mo each)

## 23. World-Class Roadmap

### Phase 1: Security & Foundation (Week 1) → Score 50
- Fix auth bypass default
- Disable dev login in production
- Remove btoa() password function
- Remove Docker hardcoded secrets
- Install dependencies + verify build
- Wire contact form
- Integrate Razorpay

### Phase 2: Core Features (Weeks 2-4) → Score 70
- Therapy booking flow (therapist listing → slot → payment → video)
- AI SoulBot (OpenAI GPT-4o-mini + crisis detection)
- Real blog system (Prisma models + API + migrate mock data)
- Real course system (Prisma models + API + enrollment)
- Admin dashboard with real data
- Practitioner dashboard with real data

### Phase 3: Production Hardening (Weeks 5-7) → Score 85
- Sentry + PostHog
- Replace stub services (email, cache, storage, queue)
- Unit tests (Vitest, 60% coverage)
- E2E tests (Playwright, 10 critical journeys)
- Per-role rate limiting
- CSRF validation

### Phase 4: World-Class Polish (Weeks 8-12) → Score 100
- SEO (meta tags, sitemap, schema.org)
- Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA)
- Performance optimization (lazy loading, bundle trimming)
- Design system documentation (Storybook)
- Internationalization (Hindi + English)
- Data encryption (AES-256 for therapy data)
- Audit logging
- Mobile app foundation

3. **Therapy Booking Flow** — End-to-end booking system
4. **AI SoulBot** — OpenAI-powered mental wellness chatbot
5. **Blog System** — CMS-backed content platform

7. **Sentry + PostHog** — Error tracking + analytics
8. **Email Service** — Resend integration
9. **Dead Code Cleanup** — Remove 90+ abandoned files

Each prompt includes: OBJECTIVE, READ FIRST, FILES YOU MAY EDIT, IMPLEMENTATION REQUIREMENTS, SEQUENCE, VALIDATION, DONE WHEN, HANDOFF NOTE.

## 25. Conclusion

### What Soul Yatri Does Well
1. **Excellent design foundation** — shadcn/ui + Tailwind + Framer Motion create a polished, professional UI
2. **Clean architecture patterns** — Well-organized monorepo, clear separation of concerns
3. **Solid auth mechanics** — JWT + bcrypt + refresh token rotation (when bypass is disabled)
4. **Health tools work** — Mood, journal, meditation are genuinely functional features
5. **Smart tech choices** — React 19, Vite 7, Prisma 7 are all current-generation tools

### What Needs Immediate Attention
1. **Security vulnerabilities** — Auth bypass, dev login, hardcoded secrets
2. **Revenue enablement** — Payments, therapy booking, course enrollment
3. **Documentation honesty** — 40% of claims are aspirational, not implemented
4. **Contact form** — Primary lead capture is completely broken
5. **Dead code** — 90+ abandoned files increase cognitive load

### The Path Forward
Soul Yatri has the **best possible starting point** for a mental wellness platform: professional design, modern stack, extensible architecture. The gap between "impressive demo" and "revenue-generating product" is **8-12 weeks of focused development** using the execution prompts in this audit.

The highest-ROI action sequence:
1. **Day 1**: Fix security (R01-R04) — 30 minutes
2. **Day 1**: Wire contact form (R05) — 2 hours  
3. **Week 1**: Integrate Razorpay (R06) — 3-5 days
4. **Week 2-3**: Build therapy booking (R08) — unlocks primary revenue
5. **Week 3-4**: Build SoulBot (R09) — product differentiator
6. **Month 2**: Everything else follows naturally

### Final Score Card

```
┌──────────────────────────────────┐

│  ═══════════════════════════════  │
```
